notes: SHIP v1.14.240 — GPU CHASSIS = 6U ON DFW02 (data-only, one MASTER_U_TABLE row; John ruling 2026-07-13). Kills the 61% red aisle. THE LOADED MASTER IS THE HEIGHT EVIDENCE — John: the Master own uniform pitch carries the same authority his rail photo carried for AUS. DFW02 places every GPU at a UNIFORM 6U pitch (U1,7,13,19,25,31): 1191 adjacent pairs, all 268 cabs, zero exceptions, then mgmt at U42 and two leaf switches at U43-U44. Six 8U nodes would need U1-U48 and leave NOWHERE for the three switches the master itself places at U42-44 — a 6-node 8U rack plus switches CANNOT EXIST in a 48U cabinet. So the 8U rule was WRONG for this site, and the earlier verdict that the 6U pitch was physically impossible data was itself the error (it assumed Supermicro fleet-wide). The Master John purged on 2026-07-12 carried this same 6U pitch and was probably GOOD DATA — recoverable by re-import. Corroboration: NVIDIA HGX H200 8-GPU names the NVIDIA BASEBOARD, not the chassis vendor; the Dell PowerEdge XE9680 is 6U with 8x HGX H100/H200 SXM5 (Dell spec 263.2mm), and CoreWeave publicly bought thousands of Dell PowerEdge servers for exactly this workload. MEASURED on the real MASTER-US-CENTRAL-DFW02-BRUTAL.xlsx through the SHIPPED detector: under 8U, 1435/2347 devices (61.1%) flagged in U-collision across 240/268 racks; under 6U, 2/2347 (0.1%) in ONE rack — and that one is a GENUINE defect, not noise (c1:002, below). SCOPE: the new row is keyed /hgx.*8-gpu/ — the 8-GPU baseboard form specifically. Guard-tested: every OTHER HGX/DGX spelling is untouched (TST99 H100-HGX still resolves 8U; GB300-NVL72 still 1U; nvlink/nvl- still excluded; in-name NRU still wins). The Supermicro SYS-821GE 8U row (John photo + vendor spec, AUS hardware) stays intact behind it. HEIGHT IS PER-SITE, SCOPED TO THE MASTER AS LOADED (John ruling 2): if a future import ever emits this same model string with CONFLICTING height evidence, THAT import flags SITE CHASSIS MISMATCH at load time. NOT BUILT — John: we rule on it when it actually exists, not before. No hypothetical-site design work in this batch. STILL FLAGGED GOLD, NOT GUESSED: VAST DBox (x50) — John has now been asked three times and the answer keeps arriving as an unstruck template [Lightspeed 2U / Ceres 1U]. The master geometry CANNOT settle it either: DFW02 storage cabs run a uniform 4U pitch (AS-2125 @U1, VAST @U5, AS-2125 @U9 ...) which accommodates BOTH a 1U Ceres and a 2U Lightspeed. It is COLLISION-NEUTRAL either way, so it does NOT block the device pass — it renders gold hatch until John eyeballs one. Also still gold: 11 blank-model rows. DFW02 now resolves 97.8% of hosts. Gates: node --check x4 inline blocks + sw.js, CSS braces 3856/3856, CRLF preserved, three-stamp .239->.240, table verified against the exact DFW02 model strings (0 mismatches), guards PASS, full DFW02 replay through the shipped phantom_rackGeometry. ?legacy=1 unaffected (data-only). DEVICE-VERIFY (.238 + .239 + .240, batch of 3, HARD STOP): import the DFW02 master, then — (1) the aisle is now MOSTLY CLEAN: expect NO red except c1:002, and gold ONLY on the 50 VAST DBox + 11 blank-model rows. (2) c1:002 is THE verify target — it trips BOTH new features at once: gpu-c1-001-01 is a 7th GPU (every other rack has 6), NAMED for cab c1:001 but FILED in c1:002, sitting at U37-U42 and OVERLAPPING mgmt-c1-002 (SN2201) at U42. Expect HOSTNAME/LOCATION MISMATCH *and* U-SPAN CONFLICT WITH mgmt-c1-002, BOTH devices flagged, and — the .238 occlusion fix — BOTH VISIBLE SIDE BY SIDE IN SPLIT LANES, neither erased. (3) a clean rack (any of the other 267) renders UNFLAGGED — no red, no gold, no warn glyph on its chip. (4) carry-forward, never yet exercised on a populated master: real racks + real hostnames render; RU-less hosts show the gold NOT PLACEABLE callout; RACKED/PENDING badges visibly distinct (teal vs slate). (5) ?legacy=1 unchanged.
